title: Executive Order 9253—Extension of the Provisions of Executive Order No. 9001 of December 27, 1941, to Contracts of the Immigration and Naturalization Service, Department of Justice

flags.justification: Extends an already-established delegated authority (EO 9001) to a new agency; creates no new authority.
No new ambiguity is created; the order extends an existing, already-defined framework.
Single, narrow purpose: extending an existing contracting authority to one additional agency.
The order is explicitly 'subject to the limitations and regulations contained in' EO 9001, incorporating existing, defined standards rather than creating new ones.
A narrow technical extension inherits the parent order's incentive structure rather than creating a new one.
Standing-procedural-framework category applies: this extends an existing wartime contracting authority, revised through ordinary reference rather than requiring independent reauthorization.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
This narrow extension inherits enforcement infrastructure from the parent order (EO 9001) rather than needing to re-establish it.
A narrow technical extension of an existing framework to one additional agency; no new authority claim or precedent.
Extends INS's own contracting authority; does not redirect another agency's mandate.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created; the order extends coverage rather than carving from it.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/18 = 0.0%. Among the narrowest, most technical orders in the batch.
